question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. Multiple reliable sources confirm that CPI rose by 0.3% monthly (seasonally adjusted) in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. The market question asks if CPI will rise MORE than 0.8%, and with an actual increase of only 0.3%, the market must resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased 0.3% monthly
Data already released on scheduled date (April 10, 2026)
Multiple credible sources confirm the same figure
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (though rare for such magnitude)
Market may not have incorporated the official release yet
Liquidity risk if trying to trade large positions
